[object PointerEvent]-iip-base-year-revi.title: IIP base year revised from 2011-12 → 2022-23.
[object PointerEvent]-iip-base-year-revi.text: IIP base year revised from 2011-12 → 2022-23. [object PointerEvent]-iip-base-year-revised-from-2011-12-2022-23 IIP base year GDP vs IIP base year economy changed base year of IIP from FY12 to FY23 changed base year of IIP from FY12 to FY23 The number of items changed to 463 from 407 new addition: CCTV, vaccine, aircraft parts, water supply, and waste management. removed: kerosene, sewing machine, CFL Industrial growth Economic indicators Base year revision Manufacturing sector GDP/IIP methodology Manufacturing industry Why is the base year revised? Which products/sectors were added or removed? Difference between GDP and IIP. Important for prelims statement-based questions. how is IIP calculated? Manufacturing still has the highest weight in IIP. IIP (Index of Industrial Production) — SummaryMeasures monthly industrial growth in India. Released by the National Statistical Office. Current base year: 2011–12. Sectors in IIPMnemonic: MME Mining Manufacturing Electricity WeightageManufacturing → highest weight (~77.6%) Mining → ~14.4% Electricity → ~8% FormulaIIP = \frac{Current\ Production}{Base\ Year\ Production}\times100 MeaningIIP > 100 → industrial production increased compared to base year. Higher IIP = industrial expansion. Lower IIP = slowdown. ImportanceUsed to track: industrial growth, economic activity, business cycle, manufacturing performance. UPSC AngleImportant for: GS3 Economy GDP vs IIP Industrial slowdown Manufacturing growth Core sector analysis Relevant economy PYQs repeatedly focus on industrial growth and economic trends. test
